purpose: Full commit-history view rendered in the Git Manager content area's History tab. Loads commits via api.gitLog(repoPath, BATCH_SIZE=50, extraArgs) with `--parents --decorate=short` and optional filter args (--author, --grep for subject search, --after/--before for date range, branch ref, --skip for pagination). Computes a custom SVG branch graph (computeGraphLayout / GraphCell) — assigns each commit a lane, draws vertical pass-through rails plus cubic-bezier merge curves, with up to MAX_DISPLAY_LANES=8 columns. Each row is expandable: clicking shows CommitDetails (full message body, metadata, files changed via gitLog with --name-status, and an on-demand diff via gitLog with -p). Supports infinite scroll (loads next batch when within 100px of bottom), debounced subject/author filters (400ms), branch dropdown filter, date-range filter, and a manual refresh button.
gotchas: When --parents is added to gitLog, the backend is expected to put parent hashes inside the same `hash` field separated by spaces — the panel splits hashParts and uses hashParts[0] as the actual hash and hashParts.slice(1) as parents. If backend emits parents in a separate field, the parsing breaks silently.
Date filters use --after/--before — git accepts ISO-8601 dates only when set strictly; arbitrary user input may not parse.
loadingRef.current is a non-state ref used to guard concurrent loads — together with the loading state. Both must be reset in finally.

Loading more on infinite scroll passes commits.length as offset — but commits are deduped against existing hashes, so duplicates from concurrent reloads (filter change + scroll) won't visually appear but waste a fetch.

When branchFilter or any filter changes, the entire commits array is replaced via loadCommits(0, true) — expandedHash is also cleared. Don't forget to also dispatch GIT_SET_LOG so other panels see the new log.

Files-changed parsing relies on a `--name-status` regex `/^([MADRCTU])\d*\t(.+)$/` — git's R/C statuses include similarity scores (e.g. R100), the \d* handles that. Combined diffs (merges) emit different formats — these are not parsed.
Lane colors are read from getComputedStyle at module load — switching themes at runtime won't update the palette unless the file is re-imported.
patterns: Aggressive lane consolidation: computeGraphLayout finds ALL lanes that expect a hash and folds duplicates into the lowest lane — keeps the graph compact when branches converge.

Three connection types: 'pass' (vertical pass-through), 'parent' (down to first parent), 'merge' (curved bezier from secondary parent). Plus 'convergingLines' for freed duplicate lanes at convergence points.
Debounced search/author filters use ref-tracked setTimeout to avoid firing per keystroke.
Expandable rows render a CommitDetails sub-component below the row with metadata, files-changed, and optional on-demand diff.
conventions: Relative date helper (relativeDate) covers s/m/h/d/w/mo/y units — used in commit row dates.
FILE_STATUS_COLORS map keys to var(--color-completed)|var(--color-failed)|etc. for the file-status letters in expanded rows.
All git API calls go through window.electronAPI.gitLog — including the on-demand `-p` diff fetch which uses the same endpoint.

related: src/ui/components/git/BranchPanel.jsx (also has a graph but uses a different layout algorithm)
